headings.text: You were madeto be full.
🌱 Honestly? It's good news, and it's simple.
🌾 You have tasted this. That's the whole argument.
⛲ Every good thing is a stream. He is the source.
🍷 Inside you there are slots. They were the point.

buttons.text: Go deeperWhy the source can give without

accordionItems.question: If you want the arcIt begins full, and it ends fuller
accordionItems.answer:
It began whole. One garden where work and worship and body and people and meaning were a single life — every need met by its proper source, all at once.
            It fractured — first in Eden, then again when modernity pulled every need apart and handed each one to a separate vendor. That's the ac

accordionItems.question: Go deeperThe nervous system already knows the difference
accordionItems.answer: The good version leaves a physiological signature. A face that's genuinely glad to see you settles your whole system — the body co-regulates against another safe body in a way no screen can fake. Awe — a night sky, a cathedral, a piece of music that opens the floor under you — quiets the chattering

accordionItems.question: In ScripturePlaced in a garden, given everything at once
accordionItems.answer:
Scripture never treats human need as embarrassing. The first humans are given — a garden, food, work, marriage, place, rhythm, speech, blessing, presence — all in one frame. No separate gym, no separate temple, no separate marketplace.
            “The Lord God planted a garden in Eden… and out of t

accordionItems.question: In ScriptureThe fountain, the bread, the vine, the light
accordionItems.answer:
“If anyone thirsts, let him come to me and drink. Whoever believes in me… out of his heart will flow rivers of living water.”John 7:37–38
            “I am the vine; you are the branches. Whoever abides in me and I in him, he it is that bears much fruit.”John 15:5
            Notice what a branch do

accordionItems.question: Go deeperWhy the source can give without emptying
accordionItems.answer: Everything in the attention economy runs on scarcity — your time, your money, your finite attention, divided and sold. A spring runs the other way. It gives because it is full, not to get something back, and it isn't depleted by being drunk from. That's the structural difference between a source and

            The real thingBuilding something real and leaving it better than you found it. Raising a person. Serving something so much bigger than you that your name wi

        SLOT 05

        The need to pour yourself into something that would matter
accordionItems.answer:

        SLOT 06
        Mastery
        The need to become genuinely good at something through your own hands.
        
          
            The real thingSix months on the same hard skill, and the day your hands finally know it without being told. A craft that is actually yours. Calluses, repet

accordionItems.question:
+
        SLOT 06
        Mastery
        The need to become genuinely good at something through you
In ScriptureThe first picture of the break is hiding
accordionItems.answer:
“And they heard the sound of the Lord God walking in the garden in the cool of the day, and the man and his wife hid themselves.”Genesis 3:8
            The first disintegration in the Bible isn't a violent act. It's hiding — withdrawal from the presence that filled everything. Four communions — wit

accordionItems.question: If you want the anatomyHow a thin thing keeps you coming back
accordionItems.answer: The mechanism is simple and worth seeing once, so you can stop blaming your willpower for it. A real need gets cut off from its source and becomes a market. The thin substitute is built to be close enough to feel helpful, weak enough that the hunger returns — because in an attention economy, a satis

accordionItems.question: Go deeperWhat the brain says about changing desire
accordionItems.answer: What's solid: habits live in the basal ganglia as cue–routine–reward loops, and repetition genuinely builds them. That's the real substrate under every protocol. But that machinery governs behavior, not wanting — which is why willpower alone is fragile and prone to relapse: the brain's underlying va

accordionItems.question: In ScriptureTransformation by beholding, not by striving
accordionItems.answer:
“Beholding the glory of the Lord, [we] are being transformed into the same image from one degree of glory to another.”2 Corinthians 3:18
            “I will give you a new heart… I will put my Spirit within you, and cause you to walk in my statutes.”Ezekiel 36:26–27
            Notice the order in t

accordionItems.question: Go deeperNine re-integration moves you can run inside modernity
accordionItems.answer:
The family tableA shared meal at home with the same people most nights fills body, connection, belonging, meaning, and rest in one practice. The highest-leverage move most people have — and it's free.
            SabbathOne day where work stops, family gathers, worship happens, bodies rest, meals sl

accordionItems.question: The necessary warningEncounter without root withers — the sower
accordionItems.answer:
“Other seeds fell on rocky ground… and immediately they sprang up… but when the sun rose they were scorched, and since they had no root, they withered away.”Matthew 13:5–6
            This is the corrective to romanticizing the encounter. The seed that springs up fastest and dies is the one with no

accordionItems.question: In ScriptureCome, all who thirst — and the river in the city
accordionItems.answer:
“Come, everyone who thirsts, come to the waters; and he who has no money, come, buy and eat!”Isaiah 55:1
            “Then the angel showed me the river of the water of life, bright as crystal, flowing from the throne… the leaves of the tree were for the healing of the nations.”Revelation 22:1–2

headings.text: 🥀 The longing is real. So is the thing that broke.
🔥 You don't fight the old love. A greater one displaces it.
🏞️ Affection is the engine. Practice is the riverbed.
Start here this week
The family table
Sabbath
Walk when you can
🍞 The story doesn't end at recovery. It ends at a feast.
🤔 The honest objections.
